  1. PGFPlotsX

PGFPlotsX

0.1 Installation Notes

PGFPlotsX.jl is a Julia package and not a Quarto extension. It does require LiveTeX installation to play well with the package. In particular,

  1. need it requires pdf2svg: install via brew install pdf2svg at terminal (note that Poppler, Cairo dependencies are also installed).
  2. it requires luatex to be installed (tlmgr info luatex). Note the pdf-engine: lualatex in the project yaml.
  3. install missing .sty through tlmgr (e.g.tlmgr install colorpage)
  4. finally you need to import Pkg; Pkg.add("PGFPlotsX")
  5. When using Plots, pgfplotsx() may be indicated as the backend graphic engine. See # pgfPlot 4

0.2 Packages

import Pkg; Pkg.add("PGFPlotsX")
using Plots
using PGFPlotsX
using LaTeXStrings
# pgfplot 1
# polynomial plot thru Expression

@pgf Axis(
    {
        xlabel = L"x",
        ylabel = L"f(x) = x^2 - x + 4"
    },
    Plot(
        Expression("x^2 - x + 4")
    )
)
# pgfplot 2
# use of dot operator

x = range(-1; stop = 1, length = 51) # so that it contains 1/0
@pgf Axis(
    {
        xmajorgrids,
        ymajorgrids,
    },
    Plot(
        {
            no_marks,
        },
        Coordinates(x, 1 ./ x)
    )
)
# pgfplot 3
# surf plot. Note the dot operator

x = range(-2; stop = 2, length = 20)
y = range(-0.5; stop = 3, length = 25)
f(x, y) = (1 - x)^2 + 100*(y - x^2)^2
@pgf Plot3(
    {
        surf,
    },
    Coordinates(x, y, f.(x, y'))
)
# pgfplot 4
using Plots; 
pgfplotsx()
surface(range(-3,3, length=30), range(-3,3, length=30),
        (x, y) -> exp(-x^2-y^2),
        label="",
        colormap_name = "viridis",
        extra_kwargs =:subplot)
parametrics
tikz
